summary: The test run failed because a spec timed out (test runner reported a timeout after ~1s), causing the CI make target to fail.
root_cause: A test/spec timed out during execution: the test runner reported "[FAILED] Timed out after 1.001s.", which led to the make target failing.

evidence.reason: Shows a failing spec from the test runner with a short run duration.
Explicit message that a test timed out after ~1 second.

evidence.quote: Summarizing 1 Failure: FAIL! -- 1223 Passed | 1 Failed | 0 Pending | 50 Skipped
evidence.reason: Summarizes that the overall test suite had exactly one failing test, consistent with the timeout.
The make target test.ci returned a non-zero exit due to the test failure/timeout, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI tests locally with the same target (make test.ci) to reproduce and capture the failing test name; if the test is timing-sensitive, increase the test/spec timeout in the test runner or add retries/controlled waits for the flakey operation. Also consider running the single failing spec in isolation to debug why it exceeds 1s.
